---
title: "Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words"
---

# Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words

Range: `0887-0968`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 16    |
| stub     | 66    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                    | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                                            |
| ----- | --------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 0887  | [ACID](#rune-0887)                      | authored   | The classic four-part law of transactional storage: atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability.                                                                 |
| 0888  | `STUB` [append-only log](#rune-0888)    | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0889  | `STUB` [archive table](#rune-0889)      | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0890  | `STUB` [audit trail](#rune-0890)        | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0891  | `STUB` [B-tree](#rune-0891)             | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0892  | [backup](#rune-0892)                    | authored   | A restorable copy taken so loss is not final.                                                                                                                      |
| 0893  | `STUB` [base table](#rune-0893)         | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0894  | `STUB` [bitmap index](#rune-0894)       | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0895  | `STUB` [checkpoint](#rune-0895)         | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0896  | `STUB` [column {DB}](#rune-0896)        | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0897  | `STUB` [column store](#rune-0897)       | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0898  | [commit {DB}](#rune-0898)               | authored   | The moment a proposed change becomes durable history. Shadow: writing too early, before the world is actually ready to carry the new truth.                        |
| 0899  | `STUB` [compaction {DB}](#rune-0899)    | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0900  | `STUB` [conflict](#rune-0900)           | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0901  | [constraint {DB}](#rune-0901)           | authored   | A rule the data is not allowed to violate.                                                                                                                         |
| 0902  | `STUB` [covering index](#rune-0902)     | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0903  | `STUB` [cursor {DB}](#rune-0903)        | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0904  | `STUB` [data lake](#rune-0904)          | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0905  | `STUB` [data warehouse](#rune-0905)     | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0906  | `STUB` [database](#rune-0906)           | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0907  | `STUB` [deadlock {DB}](#rune-0907)      | stub       | A binding curse; multiple actors wait on one another forever.                                                                                                      |
| 0908  | `STUB` [denormalization](#rune-0908)    | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0909  | `STUB` [dimension table](#rune-0909)    | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0910  | `STUB` [durability](#rune-0910)         | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0911  | `STUB` [durable log](#rune-0911)        | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0912  | `STUB` [entity table](#rune-0912)       | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0913  | `STUB` [event store](#rune-0913)        | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0914  | `STUB` [fact table](#rune-0914)         | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0915  | `STUB` [failover replica](#rune-0915)   | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0916  | [foreign key](#rune-0916)               | authored   | A declared reference from one table to another table's key.                                                                                                        |
| 0917  | `STUB` [full table scan](#rune-0917)    | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0918  | [index {DB}](#rune-0918)                | authored   | An access structure that speeds reads by paying extra write and space cost.                                                                                        |
| 0919  | `STUB` [index hint](#rune-0919)         | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0920  | `STUB` [index seek](#rune-0920)         | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0921  | [isolation level](#rune-0921)           | authored   | The degree to which concurrent transactions are allowed to see one another's motion.                                                                               |
| 0922  | `STUB` [join {DB}](#rune-0922)          | stub       | A union word; separate streams, sets, or histories are brought into one result.                                                                                    |
| 0923  | `STUB` [keyspace](#rune-0923)           | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0924  | `STUB` [LSM tree](#rune-0924)           | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0925  | [materialized view](#rune-0925)         | authored   | A stored query result traded for cheaper future reads.                                                                                                             |
| 0926  | [migration](#rune-0926)                 | authored   | Stored reality changing shape without being lost. Good migrations are staged, inspectable, and reversible long enough to discover what the data actually contains. |
| 0927  | [MVCC](#rune-0927)                      | authored   | Versioned rows that let readers and writers coexist more safely.                                                                                                   |
| 0928  | `STUB` [nonclustered index](#rune-0928) | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0929  | `STUB` [OLAP](#rune-0929)               | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0930  | `STUB` [OLTP](#rune-0930)               | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0931  | `STUB` [partition {DB}](#rune-0931)     | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0932  | `STUB` [partition key](#rune-0932)      | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0933  | [primary key](#rune-0933)               | authored   | The key that uniquely names one row inside its table.                                                                                                              |
| 0934  | [query plan](#rune-0934)                | authored   | The strategy the database chose to answer the query.                                                                                                               |
| 0935  | `STUB` [read committed](#rune-0935)     | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0936  | `STUB` [read replica](#rune-0936)       | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0937  | `STUB` [record lock](#rune-0937)        | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0938  | `STUB` [redo log](#rune-0938)           | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0939  | `STUB` [relation {DB}](#rune-0939)      | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0940  | `STUB` [repeatable read](#rune-0940)    | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0941  | `STUB` [replication lag](#rune-0941)    | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0942  | `STUB` [retention](#rune-0942)          | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0943  | [rollback {DB}](#rune-0943)             | authored   | Permission to retreat toward a previously trusted state. A system without rollback mistakes hope for safety.                                                       |
| 0944  | `STUB` [row {DB}](#rune-0944)           | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0945  | `STUB` [row store](#rune-0945)          | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0946  | `STUB` [savepoint](#rune-0946)          | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0947  | `STUB` [scan {DB}](#rune-0947)          | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0948  | [schema {DB}](#rune-0948)               | authored   | The declared shape of database truth.                                                                                                                              |
| 0949  | `STUB` [secondary index](#rune-0949)    | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0950  | `STUB` [seed data](#rune-0950)          | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0951  | `STUB` [serializable {DB}](#rune-0951)  | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0952  | `STUB` [sharding](#rune-0952)           | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0953  | `STUB` [snapshot isolation](#rune-0953) | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0954  | `STUB` [sort key](#rune-0954)           | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0955  | `STUB` [stored procedure](#rune-0955)   | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0956  | `STUB` [table {DB}](#rune-0956)         | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0957  | `STUB` [table scan](#rune-0957)         | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0958  | `STUB` [tenant isolation](#rune-0958)   | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0959  | [transaction](#rune-0959)               | authored   | A unit of work meant to stand or fall together.                                                                                                                    |
| 0960  | `STUB` [TTL](#rune-0960)                | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0961  | `STUB` [tuple {DB}](#rune-0961)         | stub       | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0962  | `STUB` [unique constraint](#rune-0962)  | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0963  | `STUB` [upsert](#rune-0963)             | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0964  | `STUB` [vacuum](#rune-0964)             | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0965  | `STUB` [view {DB}](#rune-0965)          | stub       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0966  | [WAL](#rune-0966)                       | authored   | Write-ahead log: history recorded before dirty pages reach their final home.                                                                                       |
| 0967  | `STUB` [warehouse model](#rune-0967)    | stub       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0968  | `STUB` [write skew {DB}](#rune-0968)    | stub       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |

## Entries

### [0887] ACID {#rune-0887}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The classic four-part law of transactional storage: atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** The classic four-part law of transactional storage: atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability.

### [0888] append-only log {#rune-0888}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0889] archive table {#rune-0889}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0890] audit trail {#rune-0890}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0891] B-tree {#rune-0891}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0892] backup {#rune-0892}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A restorable copy taken so loss is not final.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** A restorable copy taken so loss is not final.

### [0893] base table {#rune-0893}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0894] bitmap index {#rune-0894}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0895] checkpoint {#rune-0895}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0896] column {DB} {#rune-0896}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0897] column store {#rune-0897}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0898] commit {DB} {#rune-0898}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The moment a proposed change becomes durable history.

**Shadow:** writing too early, before the world is actually ready to carry the new truth.

**Pocket gloss:** Make a proposed change durable history.

### [0899] compaction {DB} {#rune-0899}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0900] conflict {#rune-0900}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0901] constraint {DB} {#rune-0901}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A rule the data is not allowed to violate.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** A rule the data is not allowed to violate.

### [0902] covering index {#rune-0902}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0903] cursor {DB} {#rune-0903}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0904] data lake {#rune-0904}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0905] data warehouse {#rune-0905}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0906] database {#rune-0906}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0907] deadlock {DB} {#rune-0907}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A binding curse; multiple actors wait on one another forever.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0908] denormalization {#rune-0908}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0909] dimension table {#rune-0909}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0910] durability {#rune-0910}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0911] durable log {#rune-0911}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0912] entity table {#rune-0912}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0913] event store {#rune-0913}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0914] fact table {#rune-0914}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0915] failover replica {#rune-0915}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0916] foreign key {#rune-0916}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A declared reference from one table to another table's key.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** A declared reference from one table to another table's key.

### [0917] full table scan {#rune-0917}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0918] index {DB} {#rune-0918}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An access structure that speeds reads by paying extra write and space cost.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** An access structure that speeds reads by paying extra write and space cost.

### [0919] index hint {#rune-0919}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0920] index seek {#rune-0920}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0921] isolation level {#rune-0921}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The degree to which concurrent transactions are allowed to see one another's motion.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** The degree to which concurrent transactions are allowed to see one another's motion.

### [0922] join {DB} {#rune-0922}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A union word; separate streams, sets, or histories are brought into one result.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0923] keyspace {#rune-0923}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0924] LSM tree {#rune-0924}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0925] materialized view {#rune-0925}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A stored query result traded for cheaper future reads.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** A stored query result traded for cheaper future reads.

### [0926] migration {#rune-0926}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Stored reality changing shape without being lost. Good migrations are staged, inspectable, and reversible long enough to discover what the data actually contains.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** Change stored reality without losing it.

### [0927] MVCC {#rune-0927}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Versioned rows that let readers and writers coexist more safely.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** Versioned rows that let readers and writers coexist more safely.

### [0928] nonclustered index {#rune-0928}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0929] OLAP {#rune-0929}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0930] OLTP {#rune-0930}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0931] partition {DB} {#rune-0931}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0932] partition key {#rune-0932}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0933] primary key {#rune-0933}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The key that uniquely names one row inside its table.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** The key that uniquely names one row inside its table.

### [0934] query plan {#rune-0934}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The strategy the database chose to answer the query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** The strategy the database chose to answer the query.

### [0935] read committed {#rune-0935}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0936] read replica {#rune-0936}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0937] record lock {#rune-0937}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0938] redo log {#rune-0938}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0939] relation {DB} {#rune-0939}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0940] repeatable read {#rune-0940}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0941] replication lag {#rune-0941}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0942] retention {#rune-0942}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0943] rollback {DB} {#rune-0943}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Permission to retreat toward a previously trusted state. A system without rollback mistakes hope for safety.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** Retreat toward a previously trusted state.

### [0944] row {DB} {#rune-0944}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0945] row store {#rune-0945}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0946] savepoint {#rune-0946}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0947] scan {DB} {#rune-0947}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0948] schema {DB} {#rune-0948}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The declared shape of database truth.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** The declared shape of database truth.

### [0949] secondary index {#rune-0949}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0950] seed data {#rune-0950}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0951] serializable {DB} {#rune-0951}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0952] sharding {#rune-0952}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0953] snapshot isolation {#rune-0953}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0954] sort key {#rune-0954}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0955] stored procedure {#rune-0955}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0956] table {DB} {#rune-0956}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0957] table scan {#rune-0957}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0958] tenant isolation {#rune-0958}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0959] transaction {#rune-0959}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A unit of work meant to stand or fall together.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** A unit of work meant to stand or fall together.

### [0960] TTL {#rune-0960}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0961] tuple {DB} {#rune-0961}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0962] unique constraint {#rune-0962}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0963] upsert {#rune-0963}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0964] vacuum {#rune-0964}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0965] view {DB} {#rune-0965}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0966] WAL {#rune-0966}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Write-ahead log: history recorded before dirty pages reach their final home.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

**Pocket gloss:** Write-ahead log: history recorded before dirty pages reach their final home.

### [0967] warehouse model {#rune-0967}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.

### [0968] write skew {DB} {#rune-0968}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Shadow:** durable state can preserve the wrong truth longer than the code remembers why it changed.
